etymology.description: semantic 甘(甘) (gān, "sweet; delicious") + phonetic 匕(匕) (bǐ, "spoon") -> tasty
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the semantic element 甘(甘) (gān, "sweet; delicious") to indicate the concept of tastefulness with the phonetic element 匕(匕) (bǐ, "spoon") to convey the sound. 甘(甘) represents the idea of something being sweet or delicious, while 匕(匕) suggests the act of eating, thus forming the meaning of 'tasty' associated with consuming delightful flavors.

examples.chinese: 这道菜非常美味可口(這道菜非常美味可口)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dào cài fēicháng měiwèi kěkǒu.
examples.english: This dish is very tasty and delicious.

examples.chinese: 这家餐厅以其旨的菜品闻名(這家餐廳以其旨的菜品聞名)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā cāntīng yǐ qí zhǐ de càipǐn wénmíng.
examples.english: This restaurant is famous for its tasty dishes.

examples.chinese: 为了品尝那道旨的甜点，我经常光顾那家咖啡馆(為了品嚐那道旨的甜點，我經常光顧那家咖啡館)。
examples.pinyin: Wèile pǐncháng nà dào zhǐ de tiándiǎn, wǒ jīngcháng guānggù nà jiā kāfēiguǎn.
examples.english: To taste that tasty dessert, I often visit that café.
